figures.caption: Table 1: Baseline performance. Random corresponds to the metric’s lowest possible score, while Oracle to the theoretical maximum. Human performance is a realistic upper-bound derived from manual annotations. Blind guessing uses an LLM to guess the answer based on the question only, as a sanity check, while Baseline and State-of-the-art corresponds to recent end-to-end vision-language models. The subtitles are extracted using Whisper-tiny.
Figure 3: Ablation study. Qwen2.5-VL-7B’s performance depending on the input modality (left) and number of input frames (right).
Figure 4: Teams’ performance on the public leaderboard over the course of the competition.

Table 2: Final leaderboard for the SF20K Competition. Teams are ranked based on their performance on the Private Test set. The gap between Public and Private scores highlights the models’ generalization to unreleased content.
Table 3: Summary of submitted solutions. “FT” indicates whether fine-tuning was performed.
